prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(submit_swap_request student_enrollment_src_1)
(reserve_section_seat student_enrollment_src_1 section_seat_1)
(allocate_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(validate_request_for_review student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(reserve_venue_candidate student_enrollment_src_1 venue_1 timetable_slot_1)
(assign_administrator student_enrollment_src_1 administrator_1)
(confirm_venue_with_administrator student_enrollment_src_1 venue_1 administrator_1)
(release_administrator student_enrollment_src_1 administrator_1)
(release_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(submit_swap_request requested_enrollment_tgt_1)
(reserve_section_seat requested_enrollment_tgt_1 section_seat_2)
(allocate_timetable_slot requested_enrollment_tgt_1 timetable_slot_1)
(validate_request_for_review requested_enrollment_tgt_1 timetable_slot_1)
(reserve_instructor_candidate requested_enrollment_tgt_1 instructor_schedule_1 timetable_slot_1)
(assign_administrator requested_enrollment_tgt_1 administrator_1)
(confirm_instructor_with_administrator requested_enrollment_tgt_1 instructor_schedule_1 administrator_1)
(release_administrator requested_enrollment_tgt_1 administrator_1)
(release_timetable_slot requested_enrollment_tgt_1 timetable_slot_1)
(assemble_swap_case_variant_a student_enrollment_src_1 requested_enrollment_tgt_1 venue_1 instructor_schedule_1 swap_case_1)
(allocate_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(lock_case_slot swap_case_1 student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(release_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(submit_swap_request course_section_a)
(reserve_section_seat course_section_a section_seat_3)
(allocate_timetable_slot course_section_a timetable_slot_1)
(validate_request_for_review course_section_a timetable_slot_1)
(consume_registrar_fragment course_section_a registrar_fragment_1 swap_case_1)
(validate_section_against_fragment course_section_a registrar_fragment_1 swap_case_1 timetable_slot_1)
(allocate_instructor_approval_token course_section_a instructor_approval_token_1)
(assign_administrator course_section_a administrator_1)
(allocate_instructor_to_section_path1 course_section_a instructor_approval_token_1 administrator_1 registrar_fragment_1 swap_case_1)
(allocate_venue_approval_token course_section_a venue_approval_token_1)
(process_venue_approval_and_finalize_section_checks course_section_a venue_approval_token_1 registrar_fragment_1 swap_case_1)
(final_validation_and_mark_ready_for_commit course_section_a)
(release_administrator course_section_a administrator_1)
(release_instructor_approval_token course_section_a instructor_approval_token_1)
(release_venue_approval_token course_section_a venue_approval_token_1)
(prepare_enrollment_for_commit course_section_a deadline_token_1 timetable_slot_1)
(apply_commit_to_section_with_seat course_section_a section_seat_3 deadline_token_1)
(release_timetable_slot course_section_a timetable_slot_1)
(submit_swap_request course_section_b)
(reserve_section_seat course_section_b section_seat_3)
(allocate_timetable_slot course_section_b timetable_slot_1)
(validate_request_for_review course_section_b timetable_slot_1)
(consume_registrar_fragment course_section_b registrar_fragment_2 swap_case_1)
(validate_section_against_fragment course_section_b registrar_fragment_2 swap_case_1 timetable_slot_1)
(allocate_instructor_approval_token course_section_b instructor_approval_token_1)
(assign_administrator course_section_b administrator_1)
(allocate_instructor_to_section_path1 course_section_b instructor_approval_token_1 administrator_1 registrar_fragment_2 swap_case_1)
(allocate_venue_approval_token course_section_b venue_approval_token_1)
(process_venue_approval_and_finalize_section_checks course_section_b venue_approval_token_1 registrar_fragment_2 swap_case_1)
(final_validation_and_mark_ready_for_commit course_section_b)
(release_administrator course_section_b administrator_1)
(release_instructor_approval_token course_section_b instructor_approval_token_1)
(release_venue_approval_token course_section_b venue_approval_token_1)
(commit_enrollment_source student_enrollment_src_1 swap_case_1)
(commit_enrollment_requested requested_enrollment_tgt_1 swap_case_1)
(prepare_enrollment_for_commit course_section_b deadline_token_1 timetable_slot_1)
(apply_commit_to_section_with_seat course_section_b section_seat_3 deadline_token_1)
(release_timetable_slot course_section_b timetable_slot_1)
(allocate_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(prepare_enrollment_for_commit student_enrollment_src_1 deadline_token_1 timetable_slot_1)
(apply_commit_to_source_enrollment_with_seat student_enrollment_src_1 section_seat_1 deadline_token_1)
(release_timetable_slot student_enrollment_src_1 timetable_slot_1)
(allocate_timetable_slot requested_enrollment_tgt_1 timetable_slot_1)
(prepare_enrollment_for_commit requested_enrollment_tgt_1 deadline_token_1 timetable_slot_1)
(apply_commit_to_requested_enrollment_with_seat requested_enrollment_tgt_1 section_seat_2 deadline_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a course swap scenario without progress loss. I need to output only the sequence of PDDL actions that solve it, nothing else.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves solving a course swap scenario without losing progress.  
- The output must consist solely of a sequence of PDDL actions, with no additional content.  

## Analyzing Initial Setup  
- Need to identify the initial state and the goal for the course swap.  
- Objects involved in the scenario are part of the initial setup to consider.

## Analyzing Course Swap Details  
- The swap involves moving student enrollments between course_section_a and course_section_b without losing progress.  
- Both sections seem to share the same student_enrollment_src_1 initially, suggesting a potential overlap for swap purposes.  

## Identifying Required Actions  
- Need to submit swap requests for student enrollments and course sections to start the process.  
- The process includes reserving seats, allocating slots, validating, and eventually committing enrollments.  

## Checking Resources  
- Department_approval_token_1 is available for course_section_a, but department_approval_token_2 for course_section_b is not listed as available.  
- Only one approval token is currently accessible, which may affect the swap process.

## Exploring Resource Constraints  
- Only one timetable_slot_1 is available, limiting simultaneous allocations for the four actors (src_enr, tgt_enr, sec_a, sec_b).  
- Section seats (seat_1, seat_2, seat_3) are available, but allocation may require releasing and reassigning slots using release_timetable_slot.  

## Outlining Swap Process  
- Need request_ready_for_review for enrollments and sections, starting with submit_swap_request, reserve_section_seat, allocate_timetable_slot, and validate_request_for_review.  
- Actions like confirm_venue_with_administrator or claim_venue_with_document require preliminary steps, such as reserve_venue_candidate, to ensure feasibility.  

## Evaluating Approval Paths  
- Both sections have attached department approval tokens, suggesting a potential path using department constraints.  
- Supporting_document_1 and available admin could support special approval routes, like transfer_venue_claim_with_document, for sections.

## Evaluating Resource Availability  
- Only one administrator is available, requiring sequential assignment to enrollments and sections using release_administrator for reuse.  
- With only one timetable_slot_1 and three section seats for four actors, serial allocation and release are necessary to manage constraints.  

## Assessing Commitment Process  
- Committing enrollments or sections releases seats, as apply_commit_to_source_enrollment_with_seat and similar actions make seats available again.  
- Only one deadline_token_1 exists, suggesting enrollments and sections must be processed sequentially to meet commitment requirements.  

## Planning Actor Preparation  
- All actors (src_enr, tgt_enr, sec_a, sec_b) need request_ready_for_review, involving swap_request_received, seat reservation, and timetable slot linking.  
- Sections may use special approval paths, leveraging attached department_approval_tokens, to streamline the process.

## Examining Enrollment Readiness  
- Requesting review for source and target enrollments (src, tgt) requires swap_request_received, active seat reservation, and a linked timetable slot.  
- Actions like confirm_venue_with_administrator and confirm_instructor_with_administrator ensure feasibility, using assigned administrators and preliminary reservations.  

## Managing Resource Sequencing  
- Only one timetable slot, administrator, and deadline token are available, necessitating sequential processing of enrollments and sections.  
- Seat reservations (e.g., seat1 for src_enr, seat2 for tgt_enr, seat3 for a section) must be managed, with commits releasing seats for reuse.  

## Detailing Commitment Steps  
- Committing enrollments or sections requires ready_for_enrollment_commit, linked slots, and a deadline, with actions like apply_commit_to_source_enrollment_with_seat making seats available again.  
- All commit actions necessitate a reserved seat and deadline, ensuring sequential handling due to limited resources.  

## Preparing Sections for Review  
- Sections (e.g., sec_a) need request_ready_for_review, involving seat reservation, slot allocation, and validation, similar to enrollments.  
- Actions like consume_registrar_fragment for sections require case association, linked slots, and available registrar fragments, ensuring proper sequencing.

## Managing Enrollment Feasibility  
- Validating requests for review ensures enrollments like requested_enrollment_tgt_1 are ready, involving linked timetable slots and feasibility checks.  
- Actions such as reserve_instructor_candidate and confirm_instructor_with_administrator prepare enrollments by reserving instructors and confirming feasibility, releasing administrators afterward.  

## Coordinating Resource Allocation  
- Timetable slots must be released from one enrollment (e.g., tgt) before reallocating to another (e.g., src), ensuring availability for subsequent actions.  
- Locking case slots requires the enrollment to have a linked slot, as seen with student_enrollment_src_1, maintaining case readiness.  

## Preparing Sections for Review  
- Sections like course_section_a need swap requests and seat reservations (e.g., section_seat_3) to be ready for review, similar to enrollments.  
- Timetable slot allocation for sections requires releasing the slot from prior enrollments, ensuring availability for linking to the section.

## Reserving Section Resources  
- Section_b needs a reserved seat, like reserving section_seat_3, ensuring availability for later steps.  
- Timetable slot allocation for section_b requires releasing the slot from section_a, as it remains linked post-commit.  

## Allocating Slots and Validating  
- Releasing the timetable slot from section_a makes it available for allocation to section_b, ensuring has_linked status updates.  
- Validating section_b for review involves linking the slot and checking prerequisites, marking it request_ready.  

## Processing Section Approvals  
- Consuming registrar_fragment_2 for section_b requires request readiness and case association, updating fragment availability.  
- Allocating instructor and venue approval tokens for section_b ensures resources are available for final checks and validation.  

## Finalizing Section Commitments  
- Section_b undergoes venue approval and final validation, marking it ready for commit with checks_complete status.  
- Committing section_b will free seat3, but timing can align with other commitments like src and tgt enrollments.  

## Preparing Enrollment Commitments  
- Source and target enrollments (src, tgt) need ready_sec_commit, achievable after feasibility and case slot locking.  
- Commit actions for src and tgt, like commit_enrollment_source, require venue confirmation and case readiness, without additional prerequisites.

## Managing Section Approvals  
- Course_section_a undergoes validation against registrar_fragment_1, ensuring prerequisites are met for review.  
- Instructor and venue approval tokens are allocated for course_section_a, followed by final section checks and readiness for commit.  

## Finalizing Section Commitments  
- Course_section_a is marked ready for commit after venue approval, maintaining has_linked status with timetable_slot_1.  
- Resources like administrator, instructor, and venue tokens are released for course_section_a after commitment, freeing seat3 and deadline token.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
